---
title: "Data & Analysis Track"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
Self-paced track · 8 topics · novice → advanced
:::

[← All self-paced tracks](../tracks.qmd){.track-back}

Work through these on your own, in order or by the topic you need. Each page has a short overview, two hands-on practice activities, a readiness quiz, recommended readings, and useful resources. Everything assumes you've done **[Workshop 1](../workshop1.qmd)** (prompting and the Drexel data rules).

## Difficulty levels

[Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} &nbsp; getting started — no prior AI-for-data experience needed.
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} &nbsp; comfortable with some coding or analysis.
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} &nbsp; experienced analysts auditing AI-built work.

## Suggested path

| # | Topic | Level | You'll be able to… |
|---|-------|-------|--------------------|
| 1 | [Data Analysis Basics](data-analysis-basics.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | Ask questions of a dataset and get a checkable first answer |
| 2 | [Data Cleaning, Recoding & Codebooks](data-cleaning.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | Clean messy data and document it with AI help |
| 3 | [Coding with AI](coding-with-ai.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Generate, explain, and debug R/Python/Stata code you review |
| 4 | [Statistical Interpretation across Stata, R & Python](statistical-interpretation.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Read output and translate models between languages |
| 5 | [AI-Assisted Web & Dashboards](web-and-dashboards.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Turn data into a shareable dashboard or web page |
| 6 | [Image Generation & Annotation](image-generation-annotation.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Make graphics responsibly and label images for analysis |
| 7 | [Qualitative Data Analysis](qualitative-analysis.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Assist coding and theming without outsourcing interpretation |
| 8 | [Data Analysis Advanced](data-analysis-advanced.qmd) | [Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} | Scaffold pipelines and audit their subtle failure modes |

## Not sure where to start?

Answer a few questions to find your starting point in this track.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions":[{"q":"You ask an AI tool to calculate the average age of participants in a dataset. How should you verify the result?","options":["Compute the average yourself in a spreadsheet or script and compare","Ask the AI 'are you sure?' and accept its confirmation","Check if the number looks reasonable and move on","Assume it's correct because AI is good at math"],"answer":0,"explain":"If you already verify AI results against the source data, you're ready for moderate topics like Coding with AI (topic 3). If this is new, start with Data Analysis Basics (topic 1)."},{"q":"What's the first step when AI produces a cleaned version of your dataset?","options":["Check a sample of rows against the original to confirm changes are correct","Save it immediately — cleaning is straightforward","Ask the AI to confirm nothing was lost","Run the analysis and see if results look right"],"answer":0,"explain":"Spot-checking AI work is essential. If this is second nature, start at topic 3 or beyond. If you'd like to build this habit, begin at topic 2 (Data Cleaning)."},{"q":"A colleague shares R code that AI wrote to join two datasets. What should you check before running it?","options":["Whether the join key is correct and how it handles non-matching rows","Nothing — if it runs without errors, it's fine","Only the output table's row count","Whether the AI model used was the newest version"],"answer":0,"explain":"If you can evaluate join logic and edge cases, try the Advanced topic (topic 8). If code review is unfamiliar, start at topic 1 and work up to Coding with AI (topic 3)."}]}
</script>
</div>

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules apply throughout
Use **public or non-sensitive** data for every activity, and match any tool to Drexel's data classification. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
